_comment: State Tax Treatment of Retirement Income — State income tax treatment of federal retirement income, property tax exemptions, and veteran-specific benefits by state. Version 1.4. Expand with additional states as needed. Data is general guidance — user should verify current state law.
_disclaimer: State tax law changes frequently. This file provides general guidance for planning purposes only. Users should verify current rules with their state tax authority or a qualified tax advisor.
_retiree_guidance: Military retirees should reference both 'income_tax' (retirement pay treatment, Social Security, tax brackets) and 'veteran_benefits' (property tax exemptions, disability-based benefits) for a complete state-level picture. The two sections are complementary — 'income_tax' covers all retirement income regardless of VA status, while 'veteran_benefits' covers VA disability-related property and housing exemptions.

income_tax.note: Alaska has no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Senior Citizen Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: First $150,000 of assessed value — mandatory statewide minimum
exemptions.note: Mandatory under AS 29.45.030(e). Municipalities may increase above $150,000.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Alaska. No state income tax. Benefits administered at municipal level with statewide minimum. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Mandatory exemption of first $150,000 of assessed value on primary residence for qualifying disabled veterans. Municipalities may enact additional exemptions above this amount.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.exemption_amount_note: Statewide mandatory minimum under AS 29.45.030(e). Some municipalities offer additional optional exemptions under AS 29.45.050.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.municipal_optional_note: Example: Matanuska-Susitna Borough offers additional $129,720 optional exemption on top of $150,000 mandatory

eligibility.rating_note: Service-connected disability rated 50% or more by VA or branch of service
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Statute references disability 'rated as 50 percent or more' — IU not explicitly addressed; recommend verification with borough assessor
eligibility.honorable_note: Discharge must be 'under a condition that is not dishonorable'
eligibility.residency_note: Must be AK resident for entire year prior to exemption year

occupancy_requirements.ownership_date: Must own and occupy before January 1 of assessment year
occupancy_requirements.days_note: Subsequent years require minimum 185 days occupancy as primary residence
occupancy_requirements.no_other_note: Cannot own other property receiving residential, senior citizen, or disabled veteran exemption

documentation: Current VA disability rating letter (dated within one year)
Trust documentation if property is in a trust

survivor_conditions.age_note: Widow/widower must be at least 60 years of age at time of veteran's death

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is exempt from Alabama income tax (Ala. Code 40-18-20)

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Alabama does not tax Social Security benefits

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption (Age 65+ or P&T Disabled)
exemptions.eligibility: Alabama resident age 65+ or permanently and totally disabled (regardless of age)
exemptions.income_limit_note: No income limit for P&T disabled; income limits apply for age-only qualification
exemptions.note: Exempt from all state, county, and city ad valorem property taxes on homestead and adjacent land up to 160 acres

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Alabama. P&T disabled veterans qualify for full homestead exemption (same as age 65+ exemption but with no income limit). SAH grant homes separately exempt. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Full exemption from all state, county, and city property taxes on homestead (primary residence and up to 160 acres). P&T disabled veterans qualify at any age with no income limit.

eligibility.rating_note: Must be permanently and totally disabled — Alabama uses general P&T standard, not specific VA rating percentage
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Verify with county — statute references permanent and total disability

application.note: Must own and occupy as primary residence on first day of tax year

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.survivor_note: General homestead exemption does not automatically transfer to surviving spouse — spouse must independently qualify (e.g., by age 65+)

sah_housing_exemption.description: Home acquired through VA Specially Adapted Housing grant is fully exempt from all Alabama property taxes regardless of value

military_retirement.note: Arizona fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Arizona does not tax Social Security benefits

exemptions.name: Widow/Widower/Disabled Person Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: CPI-indexed annually. Assessed value cap of $36,865 applies to non-veteran disabled/widow(er) applicants.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Arizona. Major expansion effective January 1, 2026 via SB 1749 (Chapter 247). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_full_exemption.description: Full exemption from all property taxes on primary residence for veterans with 100% service-connected disability

eligibility.rating_note: Based on combined service-connected disability rating per VA, NOT compensation level
eligibility.iu_note: CRITICAL: IU veterans with combined ratings below 100% do NOT qualify for the full exemption. Maricopa County Assessor confirms combined service-connected rating is the controlling factor.

excluded_from_income: Military pensions (all types — expanded by SB 1749 from disability pensions only)
income_requirements.note: Income limits apply even for 100% service-connected full exemption

ownership_requirements.spouse_note: Property jointly owned with spouse is treated as if owned solely by the veteran for full exemption purposes

application.window: First Monday in January through March 1
documentation: VA Benefits Summary Letter showing 100% service-connected disability

survivor_conditions.property_note: Surviving spouse exemption applies to the property itself — if spouse sells, exemption ends
survivor_conditions.effective_note: Surviving spouse can first claim continued exemption beginning January 2027, only if veteran received full exemption in 2026 and passed away during 2026

disabled_veteran_full_exemption.authority: ARS § 42-11111 as amended by SB 1749 (Laws 2025, Chapter 247, signed June 27, 2025)

disabled_veteran_partial_exemption.description: Assessed value reduction prorated by disability percentage for veterans with service-connected or nonservice-connected disability
disabled_veteran_partial_exemption.exemption_amount_note: CPI-indexed annually. Amount is multiplied by veteran's disability percentage.

property_value_limit.note: Property assessment cap removed for veterans by SB 1749 (still applies to non-veteran disabled/widow(er) applicants)

application.window: First Monday in January through March 1

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

military_retirement.note: California exempts up to $20,000 of military retired pay or SBP annuities from state income tax for qualifying taxpayers. Enacted June 27, 2025 (SB 132). Sunsets before tax year 2030 unless renewed.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: California does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates from 1% to 13.3% (highest in nation)

exemptions.name: Homeowners Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: $7,000 reduction in assessed value for owner-occupied primary residence
exemptions.note: Available to all homeowners; filed once with county assessor

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for California. Two-tier disabled veteran exemption indexed annually for inflation. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veterans_exemption.description: Reduces assessed value of primary residence for veterans rated 100% disabled or compensated at 100% due to IU. Two tiers: basic (no income test) and low-income (annual income verification).
disabled_veterans_exemption.inflation_note: Both exemption amounts and income limit compounded annually by BOE inflation factor

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans compensated at 100% rate due to individual unemployability qualify
eligibility.alternate_criteria: Blind in both eyes or loss of use of two or more limbs
eligibility.wartime_note: Must have served during designated period per R&TC 205.5(b)(1)(A)

application.deadline: By December 31 of the year exemption is sought for full amount
application.late_filing: 85% of exemption for first late year; retroactive filings up to 8 years

survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouse of qualified veteran or veteran who died of service-connected cause

veterans_exemption.description: General veterans exemption of $4,000 from assessed value for qualifying veterans with limited property
veterans_exemption.note: Very limited eligibility due to low property ownership caps. Most homeowners exceed the limit.

sb_296.would_do: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans on primary residence, effective Jan 1, 2026 through Jan 1, 2036
sb_296.note: Bipartisan support; passed Senate 38-0 before being placed in Assembly suspense

military_retirement.note: Colorado allows subtraction of military retirement pay from taxable income with age-based limits

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Colorado allows subtraction of Social Security benefits from taxable income with limits

income_tax.note: Colorado has a flat income tax rate of 4.4% (2024)

exemptions.name: Senior Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual value for seniors 65+ who have owned and occupied home for 10+ years

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exemptions for Colorado. Applications submitted to county assessor. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual value of primary residence for qualifying disabled veterans
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.max_tax_savings_note: Maximum exemption is 50% of $200,000 = $100,000 reduction in taxable value

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans with Individual Unemployability (TDIU) status qualify if rated at least 70% but compensated at 100%
eligibility.service_requirement: 24 months active duty (including National Guard called to active service)
eligibility.dhs_note: 100% permanent disability through DHS or military branch disability retirement also qualifies

residency_requirements.occupancy_note: Must live in property at least 51% of the year and pay Colorado resident taxes

application.file_with: County Assessor where property is located
documentation: State application form (from DOLA Property Tax Division or county assessor)
VA Benefit Summary Letter (Award Letter) showing 100% P&T status
Letter should be dated within last 365 days
application.renewal: Not required annually unless moved or VA status changes; must reapply at new address

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.state_reimbursement_note: State reimburses county treasurer for lost revenue

pending_initiative.initiative: 2025-2026 #49 (Property Tax Exemption for 100% P&T Disabled Veterans)
pending_initiative.proposed_change: Full (100%) property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T or TDIU veterans
pending_initiative.note: Current partial exemption would be replaced with full exemption if passed

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.authority: Colorado Constitution Article X, Section 3.5; Colorado Revised Statutes

gold_star_spouse_exemption.description: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual value for Gold Star spouses

eligibility.condition: Surviving spouse of service member who died in the line of duty
eligibility.documentation: Death gratuity from Department of Defense, or DD Form 1300 (Report of Casualty)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

ss_income.note: District of Columbia does not tax Social Security benefits

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable

military_retirement.note: DC residents pay tax on military retirement pay

dic.note: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) is tax-free

sbp.note: Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) annuities are excluded from DC gross income

income_tax.note: DC has graduated income tax rates up to 9.75%

exemptions.name: Homestead Deduction
exemptions.amount_note: Standard homestead deduction for 2026 — reduces assessed value
exemptions.note: Basic homestead deduction available to all owner-occupied primary residences

exemptions.name: Senior Citizen/Disabled Tax Relief
exemptions.note: Income limits apply

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for District of Columbia. All require documentation from VA. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

veterans_homestead_deduction.description: Large assessed value deduction for 100% P&T disabled veterans — reduces assessed value before computing property tax
veterans_homestead_deduction.deduction_amount_note: Amount may be adjusted annually — verify with DC OTR for current tax year

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans paid at 100% disability rating level due to Individual Unemployability (IU) also qualify
eligibility.ownership_note: Veteran must maintain at least 50% ownership of property as shown by deed
eligibility.domicile_note: Veteran must be domiciled in DC — the property must be principal residence
eligibility.dwelling_units_note: Property must have no more than 5 dwelling units

income_limit.note: Total household federal AGI cannot exceed the Senior/Disabled Tax Relief income limit for the applicable tax year

veterans_homestead_deduction.replaces_note: Properties receiving Veterans' Homestead Deduction are NOT eligible for standard Homestead Deduction, Senior Citizen/Disabled Tax Relief, or Assessment Cap Credit

application.file_with: District of Columbia Office of Veterans Affairs
application.timing_full_year: October 1 to March 31 for full tax year benefit
application.timing_half_year: April 1 to September 30 for half-year benefit on second-half tax bill
application.ongoing: Once approved, continues in future years if property continues to qualify

active_duty_members.documentation_required: DD-2058 (State of Legal Residence Certificate) showing DC as domicile

veterans_homestead_deduction.survivor_note: Standard veteran deduction does not automatically transfer to surviving spouse — separate surviving spouse provisions may apply

pending_legislation.bill: Disabled Veterans Complete Property Tax Exemption Amendment Act of 2025
pending_legislation.status: Referred to Committee on Business and Economic Development as of November 2025
pending_legislation.note: If enacted, would expand benefits beyond current deduction

veterans_homestead_deduction.authority: D.C. Law 22-283 (Disabled Veterans Homestead Exemption Amendment Act of 2018)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

income_tax.note: Florida has no state income tax. No tax on FERS pension, TSP withdrawals, SS, or any retirement income.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Standard homestead exemption of up to $50,000 for primary residence. First $25,000 applies to all property taxes; additional $25,000 applies to non-school taxes only.

exemptions.name: Save Our Homes Assessment Cap
exemptions.note: Annual assessment increases capped at 3% for homestead properties

exemptions.name: Senior Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Additional exemption for seniors age 65+ with income below threshold. Amount varies by county.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exemptions for Florida. All require documentation from VA. Florida requires homestead exemption as foundation for most veteran benefits. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

total_permanent_disability_exemption.description: Total exemption from ad valorem property taxes on homestead for 100% P&T disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_note: Florida statute specifies 'total and permanent' — verify IU eligibility with county property appraiser
eligibility.residency_note: Must be permanent Florida resident as of January 1 of the tax year for which exemption is claimed

total_permanent_disability_exemption.homestead_note: Must also have standard Florida Homestead Exemption on the property
total_permanent_disability_exemption.commercial_note: Any portion of property used for commercial purposes is not exempt
total_permanent_disability_exemption.late_application: Late applications accepted through approximately September 20 (25 days after TRIM notice) with explanation

required: VA letter certifying service-connected total and permanent disability

prorated_refund.note: Veterans acquiring new homestead property between January 1 and November 1 may receive prorated refund of taxes paid if they held veteran exemption on prior property

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: If surviving spouse sells and moves, exemption on new property capped at amount from most recent tax roll of prior property

partial_disability_exemption.description: $5,000 exemption in assessed value for veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability

eligibility.rating_note: Any service-connected disability rating of 10% or more qualifies

partial_disability_exemption.homestead_note: This exemption is NOT limited to homestead property — can apply to any property veteran owns

combat_disability_discount.description: Property tax discount commensurate with disability percentage for veterans age 65+ with combat-related disability

eligibility.rating_note: Discount percentage equals veteran's service-connected disability percentage

survivor_conditions.effective_note: Amendment 6 (passed November 2020) allows surviving spouses to retain discount

combat_disability_discount.authority: Florida Constitution Article VII Section 6(e), Florida Statute § 196.082

wheelchair_bound_exemption.description: Total property tax exemption for veterans who are paraplegic, hemiplegic, or permanently wheelchair-bound, or legally blind

eligibility.va_housing_grant_note: Veterans who received VA Specially Adapted Housing grant also qualify
eligibility.income_note: Gross annual household income limits may apply — check with county property appraiser

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Total exemption for surviving spouse of service member who died from service-connected causes while on active duty

eligibility.condition: Service member died from service-connected causes while on active duty
eligibility.service_member_residency_note: Service member must have been permanent Florida resident on January 1 of year of death

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.
veteran_benefits._id_card_note: Florida Department of Veterans' Affairs issues a 100% Disabled Veteran ID card (call 727-319-7440) usable as proof for state benefits except homestead property tax exemptions.

military_retirement.earned_income_note: Veterans under 62 must have at least $17,500 of earned income to claim the full $17,500 subtraction
military_retirement.note: Georgia exempts military retirement pay with age-based tiers

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Georgia does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.note: Georgia moved to a flat tax rate of 5.49% effective 2024

exemptions.name: Standard Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Basic homestead exemption of $2,000 from assessed value for state taxes

exemptions.name: Senior School Tax Exemption
exemptions.note: Seniors 62+ may be exempt from school taxes on homestead — varies by county

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Georgia. All require documentation from VA. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Exemption from homestead property taxes up to federally-indexed amount for qualifying disabled veterans
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.exemption_amount_note: Amount indexed annually per 38 U.S.C. § 2102 (Specially Adapted Housing grant limit). Value above exemption remains taxable.

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans rated individually unemployable (IU) and compensated at 100% also qualify
specific_disabilities: Loss or permanent loss of use of one or both feet
Loss or permanent loss of use of one or both hands
Loss of sight in one or both eyes
Permanent impairment of vision in both eyes to a prescribed degree

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.scope: Homestead owned and occupied as residence — applies to state, county, municipal, and school taxes

application.file_with: County Tax Commissioner or Tax Receiver
application.documentation: VA disability letter, DD-214, doctor's letter if qualifying by specific disability

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.renewal: Automatic annually unless eligibility changes; county may verify every 3 years

pending_legislation.bill: SB 129 (Georgia Supporting Those Who Sacrificed for Service Act)
pending_legislation.proposed_change: Would provide FULL exemption (100% of assessed value) for 100% P&T veterans, plus partial exemptions based on disability percentage for veterans below 100%
pending_legislation.proposed_effective: January 1, 2027 if approved by voters November 2026

vehicle_ad_valorem_exemption.description: Exemption from Ad Valorem tax (annual property tax or title tax) on one vehicle owned by qualifying disabled veteran

specific_disabilities: Loss or permanent loss of use of one or both feet
Loss or permanent loss of use of one or both hands
Loss of sight in one or both eyes
Permanent impairment of vision in both eyes to a prescribed degree

vehicle_ad_valorem_exemption.license_plate_note: Recent legislation removed requirement for Disabled Veteran plate — veterans may choose any Georgia plate

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.exemption_amount_note: Amount as of current year — may be indexed
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Homestead exemption for unremarried surviving spouse of service member killed as a result of war or armed conflict

eligibility.condition: Service member killed as a result of war or armed conflict (documentation from Secretary of Defense required)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

military_retirement.note: Hawaii does not tax military retired pay or SBP payments

national_guard_drill_pay.note: Exempt up to E-5/8 years equivalent for 48 drills plus 15 days annual training

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Hawaii does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Hawaii graduated income tax, top bracket 11%

exemptions.name: Basic Homeowner Exemption
exemptions.note: Varies by county. Honolulu: $120,000 standard; varies by age and other factors.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Hawaii. Administered by individual counties (Honolulu, Kauai, Maui, Hawaii County) with varying forms and deadlines. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes (except minimum tax and special assessments) on primary residence for totally disabled veterans
totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.county_note: Each county has its own assessment rate, eligibility requirements, forms, and deadlines. Benefit structure is similar but details vary.
totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.minimum_tax_note: Parcels still subject to county minimum tax (e.g., Honolulu $300/year) and special assessments

eligibility.rating_note: Totally disabled due to injuries received while on active duty with U.S. Armed Forces. Kauai County has expanded eligibility to 80%+ disability.
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Statute says 'totally disabled' — recommend verification with county assessor for IU eligibility

ownership_requirements.spouse_note: Property owned by veteran together with spouse qualifies

totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.commercial_note: If any portion used for commercial purposes, that portion is not exempt
totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.subletting_exception: May sublet not more than one room to a tenant (Honolulu)

county_deadlines.honolulu: On or before August 19 (first half) or February 19 (second half)
county_deadlines.hawaii_county: On or before June 30 (first half) or December 31 (second half)

application.renewal: Permanent disability exemptions generally do not require annual refiling once granted, but must report any changes within 30 days
documentation: Physician certification of disability or VA documentation

totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.long_term_care_note: Exemption continues if veteran moves to long-term care facility or adult residential care home (Honolulu)

survivor_conditions.retroactive_note: Widow/widower may apply even if veteran did not have exemption during lifetime, provided veteran would have qualified

vehicle_registration_fee_exemption.description: $46 state registration fee exemption on one vehicle for 100% disabled veterans

eligibility.note: Must have 100% service-connected disability and vehicle must have current registration and safety check

vehicle_registration_fee_exemption.renewal: Annual — new exemption letter required each year from Office of Veterans' Services

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

income_tax.note: Illinois does not tax any government retirement income including military, FERS/CSRS, and Social Security.

military_retirement.note: Illinois does not tax military retired pay or any government retirement plans

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Illinois does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Flat rate of 4.95% on non-exempt income

exemptions.name: General Homestead Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: $10,000 reduction in EAV (Cook County: $10,000; other counties: $6,000)
exemptions.note: Available to all owner-occupied primary residences

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Illinois. Tiered EAV reduction based on disability percentage. 70%+ effectively eliminates property taxes for most homes. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

standard_homestead_exemption_for_veteran.description: Annual reduction in equalized assessed value (EAV) based on VA service-connected disability rating. Veterans 70%+ receive up to $250,000 EAV reduction, effectively eliminating property taxes for most homes.
standard_homestead_exemption_for_veteran.eav_note: EAV is equalized assessed value — typically 33.33% of fair market value. $250,000 EAV ≈ $750,000 market value.
standard_homestead_exemption_for_veteran.commercial_note: Portion of property used for commercial purposes is excluded

eligibility.service_requirement: Active duty, state active duty, IL National Guard, or U.S. Reserve Forces

application.permanent_total_note: Per new legislation, veterans rated 100% P&T by VA no longer need to reapply annually

survivor_conditions.note: Un-remarried surviving spouse may continue exemption on spouse's homestead or transfer to new primary residence

specially_adapted_housing_exemption.description: Up to $100,000 reduction in assessed value for specially adapted housing acquired with federal VA assistance

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is exempt from Indiana income tax. Active-duty pay also exempt starting 2024.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Indiana does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Flat rate of 3.05% (2025) plus county income tax (varies by county, typically 1-3%)

exemptions.name: Homestead Standard Deduction
exemptions.amount_note: Lesser of 60% of assessed value or $48,000
exemptions.note: Available to all owner-occupied primary residences

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax deductions for Indiana. Two deduction types can be combined for eligible wartime veterans. Homes donated by nonprofits may receive 100% deduction. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

wartime_disabled_veteran_deduction.description: Deduction of $24,960 from assessed value for wartime disabled veterans with at least 10% VA service-connected disability

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouses of eligible veterans may qualify for the deductions

totally_disabled_veteran_deduction.description: Deduction of $14,000 from assessed value for totally disabled veterans or partially disabled veterans age 62+
totally_disabled_veteran_deduction.assessed_value_note: Property assessed value must not exceed $240,000

criteria: Totally disabled (any service-connected disability) at any age
At least 62 years old with 10%+ service-connected disability
eligibility.service_required: At least 90 days in U.S. Armed Forces

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouses of eligible veterans may qualify for the deductions

nonprofit_donated_home_deduction.description: 100% deduction of assessed value for homes conveyed at no cost to a disabled veteran by a tax-exempt nonprofit organization
nonprofit_donated_home_deduction.note: If this deduction is used, veteran cannot claim deductions under IC 6-1.1-12-13 or IC 6-1.1-12-14

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouses of eligible veterans may qualify for the deductions

veteran_benefits._combined_deductions_note: Eligible wartime veterans who are totally disabled or age 62+ with 10%+ disability may claim both deductions (IC 6-1.1-12-13 + IC 6-1.1-12-14) for a combined $38960 reduction in assessed value.

pension_exclusion.note: Pension exclusion of up to $36,200 for taxpayers age 65+. Subject to income limits.

military_retirement.full_exemption_note: Maryland fully exempts military retirement pay effective 2023
military_retirement.note: Prior subtraction system: up to $5,000 under age 55, up to $15,000 at age 55+

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Maryland does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.local_income_tax_note: Maryland counties impose local income tax in addition to state tax — typically 2.25% to 3.2% depending on county

exemptions.name: Homeowners' Property Tax Credit
exemptions.note: Maryland provides a homeowners' property tax credit based on income. Income-based, not age-based.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Maryland. All require documentation from VA (Summary of Benefits Letter). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

real_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from real property taxes on dwelling house and surrounding yard (curtilage) for 100% P&T disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans rated 100% permanently unemployable (IU) also qualify
eligibility.residency_note: Veteran must be a Maryland resident

real_property_tax_exemption.scope: Dwelling house and surrounding yard including structures necessary for residential use (garage, utility shed)

required: VA rating decision showing 100% service-connected permanent and total disability
Maryland residency proof (driver's license or prior year MD tax return)
documentation.va_letter_requirements: Must show 100% service-connected P&T rating, VA decision date, and disability effective date

real_property_tax_exemption.refund_note: Refunds available for up to 3 years from when veteran became initially eligible, starting with tax year 2018-2019

survivor_conditions.dic_note: Surviving spouse receiving DIC may also qualify
survivor_conditions.annual_refiling: May be required — consult local assessor's office

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Real property tax exemption for surviving spouse of active-duty military personnel who died in the line of duty

eligibility.condition: Service member died in the line of duty while on active duty

partial_disability_credit.description: County-administered property tax credits for veterans with disability ratings below 100%

rating_threshold_75.note: 50% county property tax credit for 75%+ disability rating

rating_threshold_50.note: 25% county property tax credit for 50%-74% disability rating

partial_disability_credit.scope: County or municipal property tax only — separate from state exemption
partial_disability_credit.note: Not all counties may offer this credit — contact local assessor to verify

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

military_retirement.note: Military retirement benefits paid to retirees of the U.S. Armed Forces are exempt from Michigan individual income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Michigan does not tax Social Security benefits

exemptions.name: Homestead Property Tax Credit
exemptions.note: Credit for property taxes on primary residence. Filed with annual income tax return. Amount varies by income and taxes paid.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Michigan. Full homestead exemption for 100% P&T, SAH, or IU veterans. Beginning 2025, exemption no longer requires annual reapplication. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veterans_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes on primary residence (homestead) for qualifying disabled veterans. Eliminates all property taxes except special assessments.

criteria: 100% permanently and totally disabled as a result of military service (VA determination)
Receiving or received pecuniary assistance for specially adapted housing (VA certificate)
Rated by VA as individually unemployable (IU)
eligibility.iu_note: IU is explicitly listed as qualifying criterion under MCL 211.7b

application.form: Form 5107 — Affidavit for Disabled Veterans Exemption
application.deadline: January 1 through December 31 of the year exemption is requested
application.reapplication: Not required beginning January 1, 2025 — exemption continues until rescinded or revoked

survivor_conditions.note: Un-remarried surviving spouse retains exemption, including on homestead property acquired after veteran's death

disabled_veterans_property_tax_exemption.authority: MCL 211.7b; MCL 211.7c (2025 amendment eliminating annual reapplication)

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from Missouri income tax. Deducted on Form MO-A, Part 1, Line 10.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Missouri exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax

income_tax.sbp_note: SBP/RCSBP/RSFPP survivors may deduct up to $47,633 for 2025 tax year

exemptions.name: Property Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker)
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+, or age 60+ receiving surviving spouse benefits, or 100% disabled, or 100% disabled veteran
exemptions.income_limit_note: Income limits apply — see MO DOR for current thresholds
exemptions.note: Tax credit (not exemption) — reduces amount owed, does not eliminate property tax

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific benefits for Missouri. No general full property tax exemption for disabled veterans (only for former POWs). Property tax credit program provides limited relief. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_credit.description: Property tax credit (not exemption) for 100% disabled veterans. Maximum credit of $1,100 for homeowners or $750 for renters. Income limits apply.

eligibility.rating_note: 100% disabled as a result of military service (VA determination)
eligibility.documentation: VA letter indicating 100% disability from military service must be attached to Property Tax Credit form

disabled_veteran_property_tax_credit.note: Missouri does NOT offer a general full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans who are not former POWs. Legislation has been proposed (HB 921/HB 552) but not enacted.

former_pow_full_exemption.description: Complete property tax exemption on homestead for Missouri residents who are former POWs with 100% total service-connected disability

military_retirement.note: North Carolina taxes military retirement pay as regular income

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits taxed on federal return may be deducted from NC state return

income_tax.note: North Carolina has a flat income tax rate of 4.75% (2024)

exemptions.name: Elderly or Disabled Exclusion
exemptions.note: Exclusion of up to $25,000 for homeowners 65+ or totally disabled with income limits

exemptions.name: Circuit Breaker Tax Deferment
exemptions.note: Tax deferment program for qualifying elderly/disabled homeowners

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exclusions for North Carolina. All require certification from NC Department of Military and Veterans Affairs. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exclusion.description: Exclusion of assessed value from property taxes for 100% P&T disabled veterans
disabled_veteran_homestead_exclusion.exclusion_amount_note: Current exclusion is first $45,000 of appraised value; pending legislation may increase this significantly

eligibility.iu_note: NC statute specifies 'permanent and total' — verify IU eligibility with county tax office
eligibility.specially_adapted_note: Veterans receiving VA Specially Adapted Housing benefits under 38 U.S.C. § 2101 also qualify

co_owner_rules.non_spouse_co_owners: Each eligible co-owner gets $45,000, up to $90,000 total

application.form: NCDVA-9 (Certification of Disabled Veteran's Property Tax Exclusion)
application.certification_by: NC Department of Military and Veterans Affairs or County Veterans Service Office
application.recommended_timing: Submit to Veterans Service Office well before June 1 to allow certification time

survivor_conditions.service_connected_death_note: Surviving spouse of veteran who died from service-connected condition also qualifies

pending_legislation.bill: S 660 / H 39 (Honoring Sacrifice: NC Veterans Relief Act)

proposed_changes.amount: Lesser of $500,000 or 100% of appraised value

pending_legislation.vehicle_note: Would also exclude primary motor vehicle owned by 100% disabled veteran from property tax

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

income_tax.note: Nevada has no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Surviving Spouse Exemption
exemptions.note: CPI-indexed from $1,000 base

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and vehicle tax benefits for Nevada. No state income tax. Exemptions are assessed value deductions (assessed value ≈ 35% of taxable value). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

wartime_veteran_exemption.description: Assessed value deduction for wartime veterans, applicable to real property, personal property, or vehicle registration
wartime_veteran_exemption.amount_note: CPI-indexed annually from $2,000 base (2003). Assessed value ≈ 35% of taxable value.

eligibility.service_requirement: 90+ consecutive days active duty during qualifying wartime period
eligibility.qualifying_periods_note: Includes WWII, Korea, Vietnam, Persian Gulf, and other designated periods per NRS 361.090
eligibility.residency_note: Must be bona fide NV resident (6+ months or valid NV driver's license)

application_to.split_note: Can split exemption between multiple categories or use all within one
application_to.donate_note: May donate exemption to Gift Account for Veterans' Homes

wartime_veteran_exemption.cannot_combine_note: Recipients of disabled veteran exemption (NRS 361.091) cannot also claim this wartime exemption

disabled_veteran_exemption.description: Tiered assessed value deduction based on disability percentage for veterans with permanent service-connected disability of 60% or more
disabled_veteran_exemption.tier_note: Amounts CPI-indexed annually. Approximate savings vary by county tax rate.

eligibility.rating_note: Permanent service-connected disability of 60% or more. Combined percentages allowed (not to exceed 100%).
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Statute references 'permanent service-connected disability' with specific percentage — IU not explicitly addressed; recommend verification with county assessor

application.deadline: On or before June 15 for following fiscal year (real property)
documentation: VA certificate or military document showing permanent service-connected disability and percentage

survivor_conditions.marriage_requirement: Married to and living with eligible disabled veteran for 5 years prior to death
survivor_conditions.combined_exemptions_note: Surviving spouse of disabled veteran who is also a veteran can receive BOTH this surviving spouse exemption AND their own wartime veteran exemption

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

income_tax.note: New York exempts all government pensions (federal, state, local, military) from state income tax under the State Constitution.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from NY State, NYC, and Yonkers income taxes

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: New York does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates from 4% to 10.9% (plus NYC local tax up to 3.876% for NYC residents)

exemptions.name: STAR Exemption
exemptions.note: School Tax Relief — reduces school property taxes for owner-occupied primary residences. Basic STAR and Enhanced STAR (age 65+) available.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for New York. Three exemption types available — local option. Amounts set by municipality. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

alternative_veterans_exemption.description: Local-option exemption for wartime veterans. Three tiers: basic wartime service, combat zone service, and disability. Amounts set by each county/city/town/village/school district.
alternative_veterans_exemption.local_option_note: Each taxing jurisdiction independently decides whether to offer this exemption and at what level

wartime.max_exemption_note: Up to 15% of assessed value, capped at locality-set maximum

combat_zone.additional_note: Additional 10% of assessed value for combat zone service, subject to local cap

disability.max_note: Up to $40,000 or the product of $40,000 × latest equalization rate, whichever is less

cold_war_veterans_exemption.description: Local-option exemption for veterans who served during the Cold War period. Similar structure to alternative veterans exemption.

s1183_a74.status: Passed Senate; in Assembly committee (2025-2026 session)
s1183_a74.would_do: Full property tax exemption for 100% service-connected disabled veterans (including IU) on primary residence. Local option.
s1183_a74.note: Has passed the NY Senate in multiple sessions but has not yet been signed into law

_survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouses and Gold Star parents may qualify

military_retirement.note: Ohio fully exempts all military retirement pay from state income tax. No income limits or age restrictions.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Ohio does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 3.57% for income over $115,300 (2025)

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption (Standard)
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+ or permanently and totally disabled
exemptions.note: Standard homestead exemption shields first $29,000 (2026) of appraised value from taxation

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Ohio. Enhanced homestead exemption for 100% disabled veterans with no income limit. SB 92 (pending) would provide full exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Enhanced homestead exemption for 100% disabled veterans. Reduces appraised value of primary residence. No income limit for disabled veterans (unlike standard homestead).

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving compensation at 100% rate due to individual unemployability qualify

application.where: County auditor in county where home is located
application.filing: Initial application required; no subsequent reapplication needed
documentation: VA award letter showing 100% or IU

sb_92.would_do: Total property tax exemption for totally disabled veterans and surviving spouses on primary residence. Effective tax year 2025 for real property, 2026 for manufactured homes.
sb_92.note: Would replace partial enhanced exemption with full exemption

military_retirement.pre_1991_note: Military retired pay attributable to service before October 1, 1991 is fully exempt. Post-1991 service is taxable.
military_retirement.proration_note: For split service: divide months before Oct 1991 by total months to determine exempt percentage

pending_legislation.sb_225: Would exempt up to $17,500 for retirees under age 63 (did not pass 2025 session)
pending_legislation.hb_2050: Would exempt ALL military retirement pay for veterans (did not pass 2025 session)

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Oregon does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Oregon graduated income tax, top bracket 9.9%

exemptions.name: Senior/Disabled Property Tax Deferral
exemptions.note: Oregon has no general homestead exemption. Seniors may defer property taxes under specific programs.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Oregon. Benefits are assessed value deductions that increase 3% annually. Oregon has no general homestead exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Assessed value deduction on primary residence for disabled veterans or surviving spouses. Two tiers based on service-connection.
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.annual_increase_note: Both amounts increase by 3% each year
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.additional_pension_note: Additional $2,000 if veteran currently receives a pension and lives on the homestead property

eligibility.rating_note: 40% or more disability certified by VA, branch of service, or licensed physician
eligibility.service_connected_note: Service-connected disability qualifies for higher exemption amount ($31,565 vs $26,303)
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Requires disability certification at 40%+ — IU with 40%+ combined rating should qualify; recommend verification
eligibility.wartime_service_note: 91+ consecutive days beginning on or before Jan 31, 1955, or 178+ days beginning after Jan 31, 1955

ownership_requirements.temporary_absence_note: Temporary absences due to vacation, travel, or illness do not disqualify

application.file_with: County Assessor where property is located
application.deadline: January 1 through April 1 for following tax year (beginning July 1)
application.late_acquisition_note: Properties acquired between March 1 and July 1: must file within 30 days of acquisition
application.renewal: Not required annually for permanent disabilities; must refile by April 1 if ownership or use changes
documentation: VA disability certification or physician certification

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.prior_year_note: If VA delay caused late certification, veteran may qualify for prior year exemptions if disability existed at time of VA examination
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.exemption_applied_first_to: Home, then to taxable personal property

survivor_conditions.domestic_partner_note: Registered domestic partner qualifies same as surviving spouse
survivor_conditions.qualification_note: Surviving spouse of veteran who died of service-connected cause, OR veteran who received at least one year of maximum exemption

transition_to_credits.note: Oregon legislation proposes converting property tax exemptions to property tax credits starting 2026-27 tax year. Credit amounts: ~$475 basic, ~$575 service-connected. New participants in exemption programs may be disallowed after 2025-26.

active_duty_military_homestead_exemption.description: Assessed value deduction on primary residence for Oregon National Guard and Reserve members called to federal active duty

eligibility.requirement: Oregon resident National Guard or Reserve member called to federal active duty — at least one day of qualified service during tax year

application.deadline: On or before August 1 following end of tax year claimed

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

income_tax.note: Pennsylvania does not tax qualified retirement benefits including military retirement, FERS/CSRS, and Social Security.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from PA personal income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Pennsylvania does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: PA flat tax of 3.07% — among the lowest income tax rates in the nation

exemptions.name: Property Tax/Rent Rebate Program
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+, widows/widowers age 50+, or permanently disabled age 18+
exemptions.note: Rebates on property taxes and rent for qualifying low-income Pennsylvanians

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Pennsylvania. Requires wartime service and financial need determination. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_real_estate_tax_exempti.description: Full exemption from real property taxes on primary residence for qualifying 100% disabled wartime veterans who demonstrate financial need

eligibility.iu_note: Total disability individual unemployability (IU) explicitly qualifies under 51 Pa.C.S. Ch. 89
specific_disabilities: Loss of two or more limbs due to military service
eligibility.wartime_service_note: Must have served during a period of war as determined by VA, or received Armed Forces Expeditionary Medal

financial_need_test.threshold_adjustment: Biennial CPI adjustment (Northeast All Items CPI-U index)
financial_need_test.above_threshold_note: Applicants above threshold may still qualify if allowable monthly expenses exceed monthly household income. Real estate tax liability counted as an expense.
financial_need_test.va_benefits_note: VA financial benefits are not included in gross annual income calculation

ownership_requirements.note: Dwelling must be owned solely by veteran or as estate by the entirety with spouse

application.file_with: County Director of Veterans Affairs — State Veterans' Commission certifies financial need
application.deadline_note: Must file before tax period commences for that period's exemption
documentation: MA-VA Form 40 (veteran) or MA-VA Form 40ss (surviving spouse)
Income/expense documentation if above presumptive threshold
application.processing_time: 120 days to complete documentation after initial filing

survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouse of qualifying veteran may continue exemption if financial need is demonstrated

pending_legislation.bill_1_proposed: Would remove wartime service requirement and add graduated reductions for 10%+ disability ($7,500-$15,000 based on rating)
pending_legislation.bill_2_proposed: Constitutional amendment to expand to all 100% disabled veterans regardless of wartime service, plus proportional exemption for 50%+ disability

disabled_veteran_real_estate_tax_exempti.authority: 51 Pa.C.S. Ch. 89; 43 Pa. Code Ch. 5 Sub-Chapter C; PA Constitution Art. VIII, § 2(c)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

military_retirement.note: South Carolina does not tax military retired pay. Full exemption effective for all retirees.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: South Carolina does not tax Social Security benefits

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: First $50,000 of fair market value exempt from school operating tax for owner-occupied legal residence
exemptions.note: Available to all homeowners for the school operating millage portion

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for South Carolina. Full exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans, retroactive to 2022. Pending legislation for proportional exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes on primary residence and up to 5 acres for permanently and totally disabled veterans. Retroactive to 2022 for qualifying veterans.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.retroactive_note: Veterans applying in 2025 can claim retroactive to 2023 at the earliest (previous 2 years if taxes paid on time)

eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Verify with SC DOR — statute references permanent and total disability

vehicle_exemption.note: Two private passenger vehicles exempt from property tax for disabled veteran or spouse at same address

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouse retains home exemption and one vehicle exemption for life or until remarriage

brain_injury_exemption.note: Veterans with specific mobility or brain injuries may be exempt from all residence taxes

hb_4700.would_do: Proportional exemption for veterans with less than 100% disability — percentage equal to disability rating
hb_4700.note: Would first apply to property tax years beginning after 2025

income_tax.note: Tennessee has no state income tax on wages or retirement income.

exemptions.name: Tax Relief for Elderly and Disabled
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+ or permanently and totally disabled
exemptions.income_limit_note: Income limits apply — vary by program year
exemptions.note: State reimbursement program, not a true exemption. Homeowner pays taxes, then receives state refund.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax relief for Tennessee. No state income tax. Property tax relief is a reimbursement program — not an exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_relief.description: State reimbursement of property taxes on primary residence for qualifying disabled veterans. Veteran pays taxes, then receives state refund. Maximum market value for tax relief calculation is $175,000.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_relief.reimbursement_note: This is a reimbursement (pay first, get refunded), not a true exemption. First year requires full payment; subsequent years may use voucher.

criteria: Permanent and total service-connected disability (100% P&T)
Permanent paralysis of both legs and lower body (paraplegia)
Loss or loss of use of two or more limbs
100% total and permanent disability from being a POW

application.note: Contact county trustee for application process and deadlines

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouse may qualify for continued property tax relief

disabled_veteran_property_tax_relief.credit_note: Operates as a state reimbursement — veteran pays taxes first, then receives refund

income_tax.note: Texas has no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Residence Homestead Exemption
exemptions.school_note: School district exemption of $100,000 for all homeowners (increased effective 2023)
exemptions.county_city_exemption_note: Counties and cities may offer additional exemptions up to 20% of appraised value

exemptions.name: Senior Property Tax Freeze
exemptions.note: Homeowners age 65+ can freeze their school district property tax at current level. Other taxing entities may also offer freeze.

exemptions.name: Over-65 or Disabled Exemption
exemptions.note: Additional $10,000 school tax exemption for homeowners 65+ or disabled

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exemptions for Texas. All require documentation from VA. Texas property tax is locally assessed and administered by county appraisal districts. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

total_disability_exemption.description: Total exemption of appraised value of residence homestead for 100% disabled or IU veterans

eligibility.pt_note: 100% rating OR IU determination qualifies — P&T not specifically required
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving 100% compensation due to Individual Unemployability (IU) determination qualify
eligibility.compensation_note: Must be receiving 100% disability compensation from VA

total_disability_exemption.scope: Residence homestead only — other properties may qualify for partial exemption under § 11.22
total_disability_exemption.applies_note: Exemption removes entire appraised value from school, county, city, and special district taxes

application.file_with: County Appraisal District where property is located
application.deadline: April 30, but late applications accepted up to 5 years after delinquency date

total_disability_exemption.effective_mid_year_note: If qualifying mid-year, exemption applies immediately for applicable portion of that tax year
total_disability_exemption.retroactive_note: May receive exemption for prior tax year if qualified then

required: VA award letter showing 100% disability compensation
VA rating showing 100% disability or IU determination
documentation.alternative: VA Tax Letter or DPS driver's license with disability designation

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: Surviving spouse may transfer to new homestead but exemption amount capped at dollar amount from last year exemption was received on prior homestead

partial_disability_exemption.description: Partial exemption based on disability rating for veterans with 10-90% service-connected disability

eligibility.rating_note: Any service-connected disability rating from 10% to less than 100%

partial_disability_exemption.scope: Can apply to ANY one property veteran owns — not limited to homestead

additional_exemption_65_plus.eligibility: Age 65+ with 10%+ disability, OR totally blind in one or both eyes, OR lost use of one or more limbs

application.deadline: April 30, but late applications accepted up to 5 years after delinquency date

survivor_conditions.children_note: Surviving child must be under 18 and unmarried

donated_residence_exemption.description: Exemption for residence homestead donated to disabled veteran by charitable organization

eligibility.rating_note: Exemption percentage equals veteran's disability rating

donated_residence_exemption.scope: Applies to residence homestead donated by qualifying charitable organization

survivor_conditions.spouse_note: Surviving spouse receives 100% exemption if veteran had 100% rating, or same percentage as veteran otherwise

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Total property tax exemption on residence homestead for unremarried surviving spouse of service member killed in line of duty

eligibility.condition: Service member killed in the line of duty while serving in U.S. Armed Forces

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.
veteran_benefits._vehicle_note: Texas offers disabled veteran license plates and related vehicle registration fee exemptions — see Texas DMV for details.

age_deduction.filing_status_note: Per taxpayer — both spouses may each claim if both qualify

military_retirement.note: Virginia fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Virginia does not tax Social Security benefits

exemptions.name: Senior and Disabled Exemption
exemptions.income_limit_note: Varies by locality — set by each county/city
exemptions.note: Virginia localities (counties/cities) administer their own senior property tax relief programs within state guidelines. Actual benefit varies significantly by location.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Virginia. All require documentation from VA (Summary of Benefits Letter). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section. Rules vary significantly by state.

real_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from local real estate tax on primary residence and up to one acre of land

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans rated less than 100% but compensated at 100% via Individual Unemployability (IU), AND rated P&T, also qualify

real_property_tax_exemption.acreage_note: If locality provides exemption/deferral of more than 1 acre under Article 2 (§ 58.1-3210 et seq.), the same acreage applies to this exemption
real_property_tax_exemption.manufactured_home_note: Manufactured homes qualify even if veteran does not own the land, but the land itself is not exempt in that case

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: Surviving spouse may move to a different Virginia residence and retain exemption (HB1655, 2019). Applies without restriction.

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from personal property tax on one passenger car, pickup, or panel truck for personal use

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans compensated at 100% via IU and rated P&T qualify per VA DVS and locality administration

vehicle_tax_exemption.spouse_during_life_note: Vehicle owned by married couple qualifies if either spouse is the qualifying veteran
vehicle_tax_exemption.survivor_note: Exemption expires on date of veteran's death. Passing the exemption to a surviving spouse is prohibited by statute (§ 58.1-3668(C)).
vehicle_tax_exemption.reduced_rate_note: Localities may separately offer a reduced personal property tax rate for veterans with certain disabilities (lost limbs, blind, P&T) under § 58.1-3506(A)(19). This is independent of the full exemption and rates vary by locality.

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Real property tax exemption for surviving spouse of service member who died in the line of duty (DoD Line of Duty determination)
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.scope: Separate from the 100% P&T disabled veteran exemption — this covers surviving spouses of service members who died in the line of duty, not just killed in action

eligibility.condition: Service member died in the line of duty with a Line of Duty determination from the U.S. Department of Defense

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.effective_note: Virginia voters ratified constitutional amendment November 5, 2024, effective January 1, 2025. Expanded prior 2014 amendment from 'killed in action' to 'died in the line of duty'.
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.prior_version: Original 2014 amendment covered only 'killed in action' as determined by DoD
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.retroactive_note: Applies regardless of whether death occurred before the effective date, but exemption not applicable to any period before the effective date

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: No restriction on spouse moving to a different principal place of residence. No requirement that spouse resided in Virginia at time of death.

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.
veteran_benefits._mcc_note: Virginia Housing (VHDA) Mortgage Credit Certificate is NOT a veteran-specific benefit. It is a general first-time homebuyer program. If modeled, it belongs in a separate homebuyer_programs section.

income_tax.note: Washington has no state personal income tax

exemptions.name: Senior/Disabled Exemption
exemptions.note: Property tax exemption for seniors 61+ or disabled persons based on income thresholds

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax relief for Washington State. Benefits are income-based with county-specific thresholds. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.description: Income-based property tax reduction for disabled veterans — amount varies by income level, property value, and local levy rates
disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.benefit_type: Value frozen and exemption from excess/special levies; possible reduction in regular levies depending on income

eligibility.rating_note: 80%+ service-connected disability OR receiving VA compensation at 100% rate
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving compensation at 100% rate qualify regardless of actual disability percentage
eligibility.age_note: Veterans 61+ may also qualify under senior exemption rules

income_requirements.threshold_note: Income Threshold 3 is based on county median household income — varies by county
income_requirements.va_benefits_note: VA disability compensation and DIC are NOT counted toward combined disposable income

benefit_levels.note: Three exemption levels based on final calculated combined disposable income
benefit_levels.level_1: Highest income within threshold — frozen value, exempt from excess levies
benefit_levels.level_3: Lowest income — maximum exemptions from regular and excess levies

application.file_with: County Assessor where property is located
documentation: Application form from county assessor or WA DOR website
VA Benefit Summary Letter or DOR Proof of Disability Statement
application.renewal: Required at least once every 6 years; county will send renewal notice

disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.scope: Primary residence and up to one acre of land; mobile homes may qualify even if land is leased
disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.survivor_transfer_note: Veteran exemption does not automatically transfer; surviving spouses may qualify for separate grant program

pending_legislation.proposed_change: Would provide full property tax exemption for 100% disabled or IU veterans, plus tiered exemptions for lower ratings ($5K-$12K based on rating)

surviving_spouse_grant_program.description: Grant assistance for property tax payment for qualifying surviving spouses of veterans
surviving_spouse_grant_program.benefit_type: Grant to help pay property taxes — does not need to be repaid if residence requirements met

eligibility.veteran_condition: Veteran had 100% disability rating or died from service-connected causes

income_requirements.income_note: Combined disposable income must be $40,000 or less (VA benefits excluded)

repayment.condition: Must continue living in residence until at least December 15 of year grant received

application.contact_alternative: WA Department of Revenue Grant Administrator at (360) 534-1400

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

_expansion_note: 25 states live: VA, MD, DC, FL, TX, GA, NC, CO, WA (v1.4) + PA, AK, HI, AZ, NV, OR (v1.5) + CA, NY, OH, IL, MI, TN, SC, AL, MO, IN (v1.6). Priority coverage for federal workforce, high veteran population, and highest veteran-population uncovered states.

federal_veteran_benefits._schema_note: Federal veteran benefits that apply identically in all states. Included at root level to avoid repeating in every state entry. State entries reference this section.

va_funding_fee_exemption.description: Waiver of one-time VA home loan funding fee on purchase, refinance, and construction loans
va_funding_fee_exemption.fee_range: 0.5% to 3.3% of loan amount depending on loan type, down payment, and prior use
va_funding_fee_exemption.fee_range_note: Current rates effective through 2031 per Blue Water Navy Vietnam Veterans Act of 2019

conditions: Receiving VA compensation for service-connected disability (any rating)
Eligible for VA compensation but receiving retirement or active-duty pay instead
Active-duty service member with Purple Heart (on or before loan closing date)
Service member with proposed or memorandum rating from pre-discharge claim before closing

va_funding_fee_exemption.tax_deductible_note: Starting 2026, VA funding fees are deductible on federal tax returns
va_funding_fee_exemption.retroactive_note: If disability rating approved retroactive to before loan closing, veteran may claim funding fee refund via VA RLC

age_57_rule.description: Remarriage after age 57 does not bar housing loan benefits (including funding fee exemption)

age_55_rule.description: Remarriage after age 55 does not bar DIC or CHAMPVA benefits, but housing loan benefits require age 57+

terminated_remarriage.description: If remarriage ends by death, divorce, or annulment, DIC and related benefits may be reinstated regardless of age at remarriage
